text: Mixed runs set mixed-effects models combination set factors. primary use   run model different splits data.

title: Mixed by runs a set of mixed-effects models for each combination of a set of factors. Its primary use is to run the same model on different splits of the data. — mixed_by
code: mixed_by(   data,   outcomes = NULL,   rhs_model_formulae = NULL,   model_formulae = NULL,   split_on = NULL,   external_df = NULL,   external_merge_by = NULL,   padjust_by = "term",   padjust_method = "BY",   outcome_transform = NULL,   scale_predictors = NULL,   ncores = 1L,   cl = NULL,   refit_on_nonconvergence = 3,   tidy_args = list(effects = "fixed", conf.int = TRUE),   lmer_control = lmerControl(optimizer = "nloptwrap"),   calculate = c("parameter_estimates_reml", "parameter_estimates_ml", "fit_statistics"),   return_models = FALSE,   emmeans_spec = NULL,   emtrends_spec = NULL )

title: Mixed by runs a set of mixed-effects models for each combination of a set of factors. Its primary use is to run the same model on different splits of the data. — mixed_by
text: data data.frame data.table object containing stacked data combination split_on variables. function run separate mixed-effect models combination. Alternatively, vector filenames can passed, read sequentially fit (.rds, .csv, .dat, .txt supported present). outcomes character vector outcome variables analyzed rhs_model_formulae named list lme4-format formula specifying exact model run data split. model_formulae Alternative outcome + rhs_model_formulae approach. list lme4-format formulae includes outcome left-hand side. useful outcomes change one model next, want Cartesian product (combinations) outcomes rhs_model_formulae split_on character vector columns data used split analyses separate models. external_df optional data.frame/data.table containing external data joined data prior model fitting. Useful data contains external time series data external_df dataset behavioral variables vary neural sensor/region. Optionally, can single filename .rds file want pass filename, data . external_merge_by character vector specifying columns external_df data used creating combined dataset. padjust_by character vector list consisting one variables adjusted p-value calculated. defaults adjusting term (fixed effect) model, adjust tests given term across variables split_on. padjust_method adjustment method (see ?p.adjust) adjusting p-values. Multiple values can passed character vector, case multiple corrections added distinct columns. outcome_transform vectorized function applied outcome variable prior running model. example, outcome_transform=function(x) { .vector(scale(x)) } . scale_predictors optional vector predictor names z-scored (unit normalized) prior entering lmer model. Note scaling applied predictor regardless rhs model run long predictor model. ncores number compute cores used computation. Defaults 1. cl optional external cl object (created variant makeCluster) used computation. Can save overhead starting stopping many workers loop context. refit_on_nonconvergence number times model refit converge. Final estimates one iteration used starting values next. tidy_args list arguments passed tidy.merMod creating coefficient data.frame. default, function returns fixed effects computes confidence intervals using Wald method. lmer_control lmerControl object specifying optimization settings passed lmer() calculate character vector specifying calculations returned function. options : "parameter_estimates_reml", "parameter_estimates_ml", "fit_statistics", "fitted", "residuals". return_models boolean indicating whether return fitted model objects, can used post hoc contrasts, visualization statistics. Note model objects can get large, careful option since generate massive data object. emmeans_spec named list emmeans calls run model obtain model predictions. arguments valid emmeans can passed list structure. emtrends_spec named list emtrends calls run model obtain model-predicted slopes. arguments valid emtrends can passed list structure.

title: Mixed by runs a set of mixed-effects models for each combination of a set of factors. Its primary use is to run the same model on different splits of the data. — mixed_by
text: data.table object containing coefficients model estimated separately split_on

title: Mixed by runs a set of mixed-effects models for each combination of a set of factors. Its primary use is to run the same model on different splits of the data. — mixed_by
text: general, restricted maximum likelihood (REML) used making inferences parameter   estimates, whereas ML used model comparisons based log-likelihood (e.g., AIC).   "parameter_estimates_reml" requested calculate, models   fitted REML. "parameter_estimates_ml" /"fit_statistics" requested, models   fitted ML. Note REML ML requested, model fit twice since estimators   advantages disadvantages noted . Example emmeans_spec usage:   mixed_by(data, emmeans_spec=list(     em1=list(specs = ~ memory | noise_level, adjust = "sidak", weights = "cells"),     em2=list(specs = ~ memory * noise_level, weights = "equal"),     em3=list(specs = ~ memory)   ))

what: Sanitize a filename by removing directory paths and invalid characters — path_sanitize
title: Sanitize a filename by removing directory paths and invalid characters — path_sanitize
text: `path_sanitize()` removes following: - [Control characters](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/C0_and_C1_control_codes) - [Reserved characters](https://web.archive.org/web/20230126161942/https://kb.acronis.com/content/39790) - Unix reserved filenames (`.` `..`) - Trailing periods spaces (invalid Windows) - Windows reserved filenames (`CON`, `PRN`, `AUX`, `NUL`, `COM1`, `COM2`,   `COM3`, COM4, `COM5`, `COM6`, `COM7`, `COM8`, `COM9`, `LPT1`, `LPT2`,   `LPT3`, `LPT4`, `LPT5`, `LPT6`, LPT7, `LPT8`, `LPT9`) resulting string truncated [255 bytes length](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Comparison_of_file_systems#Limits)

title: Align a set of deconvolved time series files from voxelwise_deconvolution to a set of events in a task-related design — run_decon_alignment
text: atlas_files character vector containing filenames atlases used aggregating voxel-level deconvolved time series decon_dir directory containing deconvolved data voxelwise_deconvolution. out_dir voxelwise_deconvolution. alignments list alignment specifications, computed function. See get_medusa_interpolated_ts help arguments, see Details. nbins atlases continuous values, many bins used discretize mask values, leading aggregation bin. aggregate_by column name individual deconvolved files used averaging repeated units (e.g., voxels) single event-aligned time series. commonly, "atlas_value", lead averaging voxels within parcel mask. overwrite TRUE, overwrite existing output files tr repetition time scan seconds ncpus number cores use parallel job mem_per_cpu amount memory (RAM) allocated CPU job. Default "4g" (4 gigabytes) walltime time requested event alignment job. Default: 1:00:00 (1 hour). scheduler job scheduler used alignment. Options "slurm" (default), "torque", "local" time_audit TRUE, additional columns added output showing alignment calculated vis--vis event timing

title: Align a set of deconvolved time series files from voxelwise_deconvolution to a set of events in a task-related design — run_decon_alignment
text: function create separate R batch job scheduler combination atlas files alignments.   jobs can allocated number cores, used loop deconvolved time series files event alignment.   number cores requested alignment job specified using ncpus, defaults 8. alignments argument list containing information event-align deconvolved time series. Elements include: $evt_col: (Required) name column trial_df containing event interest. $time_before: (Required) number seconds event interest include interpolation window. $time_after: (Required) number seconds event interest include interpolation window. $pad_before: (Optional) number seconds earliest event include interpolation window      avoid missing values edge interpolation window. Default: -1.5 $pad_after: (Optional) number seconds latest event include interpolation window      avoid missing values edge interpolation window. Default: 1.5

title: Align a set of deconvolved time series files from voxelwise_deconvolution to a set of events in a task-related design — run_decon_alignment
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{   atlas_files <- c(     "/proj/mnhallqlab/projects/clock_analysis/fmri/ph_da_striatum/masks/bilateral_striatum_tight_7Networks_2.3mm.nii.gz",     "/proj/mnhallqlab/projects/clock_analysis/fmri/ph_da_striatum/masks/pauli_combined_integermask_2.3mm.nii.gz"   )    decon_dir <- "/proj/mnhallqlab/users/michael/sceptic_decon" # has the outputs of voxelwise_deconvolution for these atlases   trial_df <- get_trial_data(repo_directory = "/proj/mnhallqlab/projects/clock_analysis", dataset = "mmclock_fmri", groupfixed = TRUE)     alignments <- list(     clock_long = list(       evt_col = "clock_onset",       time_before = -5,       time_after = 10,       collide_before = "iti_onset", # censor data if we bump into the end of the prior trial       collide_after = "clock_onset" # censor data it we hit the next trial     ),     rt_long = list(       evt_col = "rt_time",       time_before = -4,       time_after = 7,       collide_before = "iti_onset",       collide_after = "clock_onset"     )   )    # run all atlases and alignments as separate slurm jobs (2 hours each, 8 cpus)    run_decon_alignment(atlas_files, decon_dir, trial_df, alignments,      overwrite = TRUE, tr = 1.0, ncpus = 8, walltime = "2:00:00", scheduler = "slurm")  } # }

title: Main worker function for setting up an analysis pipeline — setup_glm_pipeline
text: analysis_name character string providing useful name identifying analysis. Practically, influences top-level folder name group analysis outputs, well name .RData objects saved function output directory analysis. scheduler HPC scheduler system used queueing jobs. Options 'slurm', 'torque', 'local'. output_directory output directory configuration job submission files analysis. Default subfolder called "glm_out" current working directory. subject_data data.frame containing subject-level data age, sex, covariates. Columns subject_data can used covariates group (aka 'level 3') analyses. NULL, distilled trial_data looking variables vary level vm["id"]. run_data data.frame containing run-level data run condition run number. Columns run_data can used covariates subject (aka 'level 2') analyses. NULL, distilled trial_data looking variables vary level vm["run_number"]. trial_data data.frame containing trial-level statistics subjects. Data stacked long form row represents single trial given subject number total rows subjects x trials. wish, can pass single trial-level data frame also contains run-level subject-level covariates (.e., combined long format, variables different levels included columns). case, setup_glm_pipeline detect variables occur level parse accordingly subject_data run_data. ppi_data optional `data.frame` containing physiological signals (e.g., deconvolved time series seed ROIs) can used setting level 1 models PPI analysis. Specifically, `data.frame` provided, able create interaction regressors convolved event-related signal columns `ppi_data`. data.frame must include subject id, session id (relevant), run_number columns. additional columns eligible physiological signals PPI analysis. vm named character vector containing key identifying columns subject_data trial_data. Minimally, vector contain elements 'id' bad_ids optional vector ids subject_data trial_data excluded analysis. tr repetition time scanning sequence seconds. Used setting design matrices. NULL, function look tr field run_data object, specifies TR run level (e.g., varies). fmri_file_regex character string containing Perl-compatible regular expression subfolder filename within mr_dir field subject_data. drop_volumes number volumes drop fMRI data convolved regressors prior analysis. Default 0. l1_models l1_model_set object containing level 1 (run) models included GLM pipeline. "prompt" passed, setup_glm_pipeline call build_l1_models setup l1 models interactively. Optionally, argument can NULL want setup l1 models later, though resulting object functional within pipeline l1 models provided. l2_models hi_model_set object containing level 2 (subject) models included GLM pipeline. "prompt" passed, setup_glm_pipeline call build_l2_models setup l2 models interactively. Optionally, argument can NULL want setup l2 models later, though resulting object functional within pipeline l2 models provided. l3_models hi_model_set object containing level 3 (sample) models included GLM pipeline. "prompt" passed, setup_glm_pipeline call build_l3_models setup l3 models interactively. Optionally, argument can NULL want setup l3 models later, though resulting object functional within pipeline l3 models provided. glm_software fMRI analysis package use analysis. Options "FSL", "SPM", "AFNI" (case insensitive). n_expected_runs Number expected runs per subject. Used determine 2- versus 3-level analysis (FSL), providing feedback subjects unexpected numbers runs. use_preconvolve boolean indicating whether enter convolved regressors GLM estimation software (e.g., FSL FILM/FEAT). TRUE, regressors generated build_design_matrix. FALSE, onset-duration-value timing entered convolution handled internally GLM software. recommend TRUE consistency. additional list additional metadata added glm.pipeline object returned function. can useful identifiers want long-term storage -shoot functions. lgr_threshold logging threshold used determine whether output messages different severity screen log files. Default "info", produces messages, warnings, errors, debug trace statements. output concerning errors, change "error". See: https://s-fleck.github.io/lgr/articles/lgr.html details. block_data optional data.frame containing information design features task vary block level (typically, longer periods time 10-30s). Blocks superordinate trials subordinate runs. mr_dir_column character string indicating column name subject_data containing folder subject's data. Default "mr_dir".

text: function setup FSL level 2 (subject) .fsf files combinations   l2_model_names l1_model_names. FSL 2-level versus 3-level setup 2-level setup (one run per subject)   - Pass L1 .feat folders input L3 .fsf setup   - approach, copes .fsf pertain L1 cope numbers   - Requires one .fsf per L3 model 3-level setup (multiple runs per subject, combined L2)   - Pass individual cope*.feat folders within subject .gfeat folders   - folder cope numbers pertain L1 copes   - cope*.nii.gz cope*.feat subfolders pertain L2 contrasts   - Requires one .fsf per L1 cope x L3 model combination   - Example: FSL_L2.gfeat/cope3.feat/stats/cope1.nii.gz      ==> cope3 third contrast L1 feat model      ==> cope1 first contrast L2 feat model

what: Identify the last valid volume acquired in a given run. Subjects often exhibit head movement after run ends (MATLAB closes), but scan hasn't stopped This occurs because the MB raw transfer of the prior run is occurring, but does not finish before the current run Thus, truncate mr files to be 12 seconds after final feedback presentation, which is how the paradigm timing files are setup — truncate_runs
title: Identify the last valid volume acquired in a given run. what: Bug fixes in build_design_matrix / fmri.stimulus convolution
title: fmri.pipeline 0.4
text: Fix: overlapping events silently overwritten stimulus construction. construct_stimulus (inside fmri.stimulus) used assignment (stim[idx] <- values[]) building microtime stimulus vector. two events overlapped time (e.g., long-duration events short ITIs), later events overwrote earlier ones, silently producing incorrect regressors. Fixed use additive accumulation (stim[idx] <- stim[idx] + values[]), correct linearity convolution. affected normalization modes (none, durmax_1, evtmax_1). Fix: microtime bin truncation causing position-dependent convolution peaks. Floating-point arithmetic fmri.stimulus converting onset times microtime bins produce values like 2013.999... instead 2014, R truncated wrong bin. shifted events one microtime step, causing identical events different run positions ~0.4% different peak amplitudes. Fixed applying round() microtime onset duration indices. Optimized evtmax_1 normalization. Replaced previous approach N separate full-run FFT convolutions (one per event) precomputed HRF peaks via small padded windows, followed single combined convolution. also eliminates previous fragile workaround end--run events (re-convolving center run estimate “true” peak). Speedups 2-9x depending number events, number unique durations, run length. Thread microtime_resolution parameter convolve_regressor ensure peak lookups grid-phase computation always use resolution main convolution. Fix: insufficient convolution padding sub-second TRs. convolve_stimulus used fixed 20-TR padding edge-effect suppression, TR=0.5s 10 seconds — enough double-gamma HRF undershoot decay (still ~-0.02 20s). Changed 30 seconds absolute time padding regardless TR, preventing circular convolution artifacts near run boundaries multiband acquisitions.
